title: Probabilistic Ensemble Scoring over Structured Outputs

rubrics.criterion: Correctly parses dot-bracket notation to extract base pairs using a stack-based or equivalent algorithm.
Correctly identifies all unpaired nucleotide positions in each structure.
Correctly classifies each base pair as GC, AU/GU, or other, based on the sequence.
Correctly assigns energy contributions to all base pairs according to the energy model.
Correctly applies the +1 unpaired nucleotide penalty for every unpaired position.
Correctly computes total free energy ΔG for each structure by summing all contributions.
Correctly converts ΔG into unnormalized weight using exp(−ΔG / RT) with the provided constants.
Correctly computes the partition function Z as the sum of all unnormalized weights.
Correctly computes normalized probabilities by dividing each weight by Z.
Normalized probabilities sum to 1.0 within a reasonable numerical tolerance (≤ 0.001).
Correctly identifies which candidate structures contain the target base pair (1,6).
Correctly excludes structures that do not contain the target pair from the marginal probability sum.
Correctly aggregates probabilities of qualifying structures to compute p(1,6).
Maintains consistent 1-based indexing throughout without off-by-one errors.
Correctly handles non-canonical base pairs (e.g., G–G, C–C) using the 'other' energy rule.
Detects or acknowledges the length mismatch in y5 (7 characters vs. 6-nucleotide sequence).
Final numeric answer is consistent with all intermediate calculations (within tolerance ≤ 0.005).
Reasoning is coherent and links structure parsing → energy computation → probabilistic normalization → final aggregation without logical gaps.
